_note: Materiality / change-impact test corpus. Each fixture asserts the correct verdict for ONE change to an H-1B petition field (an inbound email moved a value). Deterministic — no models, no network. Each case carries a rule number, legal_basis, and an attorney redline field (empty = pending review), so the same file is both the test corpus and the review surface. Synthetic data — no real PII.

verdict_classes.amendment_required: amended I-129 generally required before the change takes effect (material)
verdict_classes.notice_obligation: a filing obligation that does NOT amend the petition (e.g. AR-11)
verdict_classes.stale: the petition copy should be updated, not necessarily re-filed (not material)
verdict_classes.constraint_violated: a cross-document inconsistency the change causes (RFE/denial/blocker)

cases.title: Wage decreases but stays above prevailing → amendment (no constraint)
cases.legal_basis: DOL 20 CFR 655.731 — offered wage must meet/exceed prevailing; a decrease can require a new LCA before it takes effect.

cases.title: Wage increases → stale (update the figure, not an amendment trigger)
cases.legal_basis: Informational — a raise does not lower the wage below prevailing, so it updates the petition figure but is not itself an amendment trigger.

cases.title: Title changes AFTER filing → amendment (specialty-occupation basis)
cases.legal_basis: 8 CFR 214.2(h)(2)(i)(E) + Matter of Simeio line on material changes — a post-filing change in position can alter the specialty-occupation basis and generally requires an amended petition before it takes effect.

cases.title: Title changes BEFORE filing → stale (same change, earlier stage)
cases.legal_basis: Pre-filing — update the petition and re-confirm the specialty-occupation basis; no amended petition because nothing has been filed yet.

cases.title: Worksite moves OUTSIDE the LCA area → amendment (Simeio)
cases.legal_basis: Matter of Simeio Solutions, 26 I&N Dec. 542 (AAO 2015) — a worksite outside the LCA's area of intended employment requires a new LCA + amended petition.

cases.title: Worksite moves WITHIN the LCA area → stale (suite/formatting change)

change.new: Building C, 500 Technology Dr, Austin, TX 78701

cases.legal_basis: Within the certified area of intended employment — confirm it still falls inside the area; a formatting/suite change updates the petition but does not amend it.

cases.title: Beneficiary home address changes → AR-11 notice within 10 days (NOT an amendment)

change.old: 1200 W Dean Keeton St, Apt 305, Austin, TX 78705

cases.legal_basis: 8 CFR 265.1 — a residential address change requires Form AR-11 within 10 days; it does not amend the petition.

cases.title: Beneficiary legal name corrected → amendment / consistency (biographic match)
cases.legal_basis: USCIS identity-consistency — corrected biographic data must match the petition and all supporting documents to avoid an inconsistency finding / RFE.

cases.title: SOC / occupation code changes → amendment (LCA correspondence)
cases.legal_basis: LCA-correspondence (DOL/USCIS) — the SOC code must correspond between the petition and the certified LCA; a change generally requires a new LCA + amended petition.

cases.title: Boundary — wage cut BELOW prevailing → amendment AND a counterfactual constraint violation
cases.legal_basis: Exercises the counterfactual constraint pass: the decrease is itself an amendment (rule 1) AND, applied in-memory, drops the offered wage below the certified prevailing wage (DOL 20 CFR 655.731) — a denial-grade cross-document violation the change CAUSES.

cases.title: Coverage — requested end date extended → amendment (extension petition) [AI-drafted, pending]
cases.legal_basis: AI-DRAFTED, ATTORNEY-PENDING. An extension of stay requires a new I-129 filed before the current period expires. NUANCE: a pure date correction (not an extension) may only be a staleness update — the attorney may split this into extend-vs-correct.

cases.title: Coverage — full-time → part-time → amendment (terms of employment) [AI-drafted, pending]
cases.legal_basis: AI-DRAFTED, ATTORNEY-PENDING. A change between full-time and part-time is a material change in the terms of employment (and can change the LCA wage basis).

cases.title: Coverage — weekly hours changed → amendment (terms / LCA wage basis) [AI-drafted, pending]
cases.legal_basis: AI-DRAFTED, ATTORNEY-PENDING. A change in weekly hours can be a material change in the terms of employment and the LCA wage basis. NUANCE: a small change within full-time may not be material — the attorney may set a threshold.

cases.title: Coverage — petitioner legal name changed → amendment / successor-in-interest [AI-drafted, pending]
cases.legal_basis: AI-DRAFTED, ATTORNEY-PENDING. A petitioner legal-name change may require an amended petition or a successor-in-interest analysis. NUANCE: a pure name change with the same FEIN and unchanged terms may be only a notice.

cases.title: Coverage — petitioner FEIN changed → amendment (successor-in-interest) [AI-drafted, pending]
cases.legal_basis: AI-DRAFTED, ATTORNEY-PENDING. A FEIN change generally signals a different legal entity (successor-in-interest), which can require an amended petition. NUANCE: a corrected/typo'd EIN for the same entity is a consistency fix, not an amendment.

cases.title: Boundary — a schema-bound field with NO bespoke rule → reverse-index stale fallback
cases.legal_basis: Coverage check (no legal verdict): lca_case_number is bound to the I-129 schema but has no materiality rule, so a corrected case number must mark the form value stale rather than silently dropping it.

cases.title: Negative — an unbound predicate produces NO impact (no false positive)
cases.legal_basis: Coverage check (no legal verdict): a field not bound to any form and with no rule must yield zero impact items — the engine never invents a consequence for an irrelevant change.
